violations.text: The gasket for the 1 door reach-in was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The cutting boards in the prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the bleach sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Establishment is to install an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

Sliced Deli Meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/29/2023
Refrigerator door gasket on the pepsi one door were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

The prep sink in the kitchen is lacking an air gap. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (tomatoes) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

Cheese, ham, marinara sauce was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
Sauces were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler fan, walls and shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
